# Verify that a non-admin cannot seal an admin-only secret. This exercises
# the seal-time guard in stageSecret: bob is in the audit log (in the
# "dev" group) but README.md was registered admin-only by `sesam init`,
# so bob's `sesam seal` must abort before producing a footer.
#
# The cryptographic defense (reveal rejecting a forged footer) is harder
# to demonstrate from a shell because constructing a malicious footer
# requires running Go directly; that case is covered by the unit tests
# TestRevealRejectsUnauthorizedSealer and TestIntegrityRejectsUnauthorizedSealer.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# Snapshot the legitimate revealed plaintext that init produced, so we can
# compare against it after reveal without hardcoding the README template
# (which changes independently of this test).
cp README.md original_readme.md

# Add bob to the dev group. He has no access to README.md (admin-only).
exec sesam tell --user bob --recipient $BOB_PUBKEY --group dev

# Snapshot the sealed object before bob tries anything; it must remain
# byte-identical after his failed seal attempt.
cp .sesam/objects/README.md.sesam original.sesam

# Bob substitutes README.md content and tries to seal as himself.
# sesam should warn about that but keep sealing for other files.
cp evil_readme.md README.md
exec sesam --identity $BOB_KEY seal
# Match the message without the log-level prefix, so the assertion is robust
# to how the handler renders the level (e.g. a `‼` symbol vs. `WARN`).
stderr 'ignoring path because user is not authorized user=bob path=README.md'

# The on-disk ciphertext must not have been touched by the failed seal:
# the swap never happened because stageSecret rejected before staging.
cmp .sesam/objects/README.md.sesam original.sesam

# Admin reveals: gets the original content, not bob's substitution.
exec sesam reveal
cmp README.md original_readme.md

# Restore the legitimate content and confirm admin can re-seal normally.
cp original_readme.md README.md
exec sesam seal

-- evil_readme.md --
# evil readme
bob's substituted content
